competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Energy Transformations
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply thermodynamic principles to analyze energy changes, predict reaction spontaneity, and understand equilibrium states in chemical systems.
competencies: Apply calorimetry techniques to measure enthalpy changes (∆U and ∆H)
Distinguish between different types of reaction enthalpies
Predict the spontaneity of reactions using Gibbs Energy Change
Relate equilibrium constants to reaction quotients and Gibbs energy

competency_groups.title: Chemical and Ionic Equilibrium Systems
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, calculate equilibrium constants, and predict shifts in response to external factors.
competencies: Explain dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes
Apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium to determine equilibrium constants
Analyze homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria
Predict shifts in equilibrium based on concentration, temperature, and pressure changes
Calculate pH and buffer capacity in ionic solutions

competency_groups.title: Redox Processes and Electrode Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Ability to identify oxidation-reduction reactions, assign oxidation numbers, and understand electron transfer mechanisms in electrode processes.
competencies: Classify reactions based on classical and electron transfer definitions of redox
Determine oxidation numbers for elements in compounds
Analyze electrode processes in redox reactions

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.description: Ability to understand the structural basis of organic compounds, apply nomenclature rules, analyze isomerism, and utilize purification and analysis techniques.
competencies: Represent organic structures using standard conventions
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules to classify organic compounds
Identify and differentiate between various types of isomers
Analyze fundamental concepts in organic reaction mechanisms
Execute qualitative and quantitative analysis of organic compounds
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry – Some Basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry – Some Basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify hydrocarbons, understand their structural characteristics, and evaluate their physical properties and toxicity.
competencies: Classify hydrocarbons into alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic compounds
Correlate structure with physical properties of hydrocarbons
Evaluate the carcinogenic potential and toxicity of specific hydrocarbons

competency_groups.title: s & p Block Element Trends (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Ability to observe periodic trends in physical and chemical properties of s and p block elements, including electronic configurations and unique behaviors.
competencies: Relate electronic configuration to position in the periodic table
Analyze trends in atomic/ionic radii, ionization enthalpy, and hydration enthalpy
Identify unique behavior of the first element in each group

competency_groups.title: Gaseous State Behavior (Formative)
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply gas laws qualitatively and understand deviations from ideal behavior.
competencies: Apply qualitative gas laws to predict state changes
Analyze the ideal gas equation and its limitations

competency_groups.title: Practical Laboratory Skills and Safety
competency_groups.description: Ability to perform volumetric analysis, salt analysis, and content-based experiments with precision, adhering to safety protocols.
competencies: Perform volumetric analysis (titrations) accurately
Conduct qualitative analysis to identify cations and anions
Execute purification techniques such as crystallization
Determine physical constants like melting and boiling points

competency_groups.title: Scientific Investigation and Project Work
competency_groups.description: Ability to design and execute scientific investigations, collect data from multiple sources, and analyze real-world chemical phenomena.
competencies: Design experiments to test hypotheses regarding water quality or chemical properties
Analyze the effect of variables on chemical reactions (e.g., acidity, evaporation rate)
Synthesize findings from laboratory testing and external research
